headline: HEAVY SLOP WARNING: SYNTHETIC PATRIOTISM FRONT SWEEPS THE FACEBOOK DELTA AS AI-GENERATED VETERANS REQUEST BIRTHDAY WISHES THEY WERE NEVER OWED
advisory: A broad, flag-textured slop system has settled over the holiday feeds, producing dense accumulations of synthetic patriotic content: rendered eagles, fictional centenarian servicemembers, and captions that instruct residents to type a single word to prove they still love their country. The Service reminds the public that no genuine veteran's birthday depends on comment volume. Engagement is being harvested at scale under the color of the flag; residents are advised to salute responsibly and verify before they emote.

regional_outlook.conditions: Heavy fake-veteran birthday activity through midday, with 'Nobody wished me' pressure systems intensifying near the sharing button. Comment sections flooding faster than they can drain.
Persistent rendered-fireworks haze reduces visibility to zero. Channels producing patriotic slideshows at industrial cadence; expect looping brass and no verifiable source.
Scattered synthetic 'true story' threads about anonymous heroes, each ending in an unrelated product link. Gusty misattribution likely.
Standing water of AI-written 'Meaning Behind the Fourth of July' articles, all restating one another. Genuine history advisories buried beneath the surface.
Tranquil at dawn, then a red-white-and-blue tablescape bloom of dishes that cannot be assembled. Craft residents advised to read twice.

specimen.status: Abundant; peaks on national holidays and any calendar day ending in a digit.
specimen.habitat: Comment-driven feeds, particularly the Facebook Delta, where it nests atop borrowed uniform imagery.
specimen.field_marks: A weathered, subtly melted face; medals in impossible arrangements; a caption claiming it is the subject's 100th birthday and that 'nobody' has said anything yet. Hands frequently have the wrong number of fingers gripping the flag.
specimen.call: Today is my 100th birthday and no one has wished me — I bet this post won't even get 1 like. Type AMEN, HERO to prove them wrong.
specimen.confusion_species: Easily mistaken for a real community birthday tribute posted by a family. Tell them apart by the source: genuine tributes name the person, tag relatives, and never demand a one-word loyalty test from strangers.

spotter_tip: If a post honors a hero but cannot spell the hero's name, salute the flag, not the algorithm — real gratitude does not require you to type a password.
tomorrow: Post-holiday runoff heavy; expect 'What the Fourth REALLY means' recap slop draining slowly through Sunday.

confirmed_sighting.report: A study reports that AI slop videos make up roughly a third of YouTube's recommended feed, with some AI channels generating billions of views and millions in annual revenue — conditions researchers link to the 'illusory truth effect,' in which repetition alone begins to feel like fact.
